channel.title: World In 5
channel.description: The world's top news stories in 5 minutes, every morning. Politics, international affairs, climate, and the events shaping our world â€” clear, fast, free.
channel.itunes:author: World In 5

item.title: World News Jun 29 — Pakistani strikes kill dozens in Afghanistan
item.description: Dozens of civilians are dead and many more are injured in Afghanistan after Pakistani airstrikes ripped through the border region, sparking outrage and escalating tensions between the two nations. The Taliban government claims that the strikes targeted innocent people, including women and children, while Pakistan insists that it was targeting militant groups. This is a critical moment in the already fragile relationship between Pakistan and Afghanistan, with both sides accusing each other of vio...

item.title: World In 5 | Jun 30 — Police units deployed across South Africa before a
item.description: Tensions are running high in South Africa today as police units have been deployed across the country in anticipation of anti-immigration marches, sparking fears of a repeat of the deadly violence that rocked the nation back in 2008. The government is taking no chances, with security personnel patrolling the streets of major cities like Johannesburg, where many shopkeepers have opted to keep their businesses closed today. The planned demonstrations are aimed at undocumented foreign nationals, an...
